title: Campaign-Style Storage Box
summary: A small brass-pulled box in the campaign furniture style. Build it once, then again — they're deliberately meant to be made as a matching set.
description:
Campaign furniture — the collapsible, brass-cornered chests officers packed for postings abroad — is where this box borrows its look: square corners, exposed brass hardware, no attempt to hide the fasteners.

At this size it's really a five-board box with a lid. The **brass flush ring pull is what sells the look** — a standard knob would make it read as a generic craft box instead of a small campaign chest.

Build one for a nightstand catch-all, or scale the material list up and build three or four as a matching stacked set.

tools.note: Or a circular/jig saw — you're crosscutting five small panels.
A right-angle attachment helps in the box's tight corners.
For a clean, straight recess for the flush ring pull.
Squares off the corners of the ring pull's recess after drilling.

materials.name: Birch or pine board, 1/2" thick
materials.note: Cut into four 8"x8" sides and one 8"x7" bottom.

steps.title: Cut the five panels
steps.body: Cut four 8"x8" side panels and one 8"x7" bottom panel from 1/2" board. The bottom is an inch narrower than the sides — that inch is what lets it sit inside the box rather than under it, flush with the bottom edges.

steps.title: Pocket-hole two of the side panels
steps.body: Drill pocket holes into two opposite side panels only — the other two stay clean-faced. This keeps every visible pocket hole on the same two interior faces instead of scattered across all four corners.

steps.title: Assemble the box
steps.body:
Glue and pocket-screw the four sides together into a square, then set the bottom panel inside, flush with the bottom edges, and screw up through it into the sides.

Check square before the glue sets — a small box out of square is obvious at a glance in a way a large piece of furniture isn't.

steps.title: Fill, sand, and finish
steps.body: Fill the pocket holes and any gaps with wood filler, sand through the grits, then stain and seal with polyurethane.

steps.title: Install the brass hardware
steps.body:
Drill a recess for the flush ring pull on the drill press, squaring off the corners with a chisel if your pull isn't round, then screw it in place with the brass screws.

Use brass screws specifically — a steel screw head next to polished brass hardware is the detail that gives away a rushed build.
